passage_title: The growth of bike-sharing schemes around the world

sentences.sentence: The original idea for an urban bike-sharing scheme dates back to a summer's day in Amsterdam in 1965.
sentences.translation: 城市自行车共享计划的最初想法可以追溯到1965年阿姆斯特丹的一个夏日。

structure.text: The original idea for an urban bike-sharing scheme
a summer's day in Amsterdam in 1965

difficult_points: for an urban bike-sharing scheme 是后置介词短语，说明 idea 的内容。
dates back to 表示“追溯到”，强调这个想法的历史来源。
in Amsterdam in 1965 连续两个介词短语分别交代地点和时间。

sentences.sentence: Provo, the organisation that came up with the idea, was a group of Dutch activists who wanted to change society.
sentences.translation: Provo 是提出这个想法的组织，由一群想改变社会的荷兰活动家组成。

structure.text: the organisation that came up with the idea
was a group of Dutch activists

difficult_points: the organisation that came up with the idea 是同位语，解释 Provo 是什么组织。
that came up with the idea 是定语从句，修饰 organisation。
who wanted to change society 是定语从句，说明 activists 的目标。

sentences.sentence: They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism.
sentences.translation: 他们认为这个被称为 Witte Fietsenplan 的计划，是应对人们所感知到的空气污染和消费主义威胁的一种办法。

structure.text: the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism
which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan

difficult_points: believed 后省略了 that，引出完整的宾语从句。
which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan 是非限制性定语从句，补充说明 the scheme。
perceived threats 表示“被认为存在的威胁”，不一定是客观量化的威胁。

sentences.sentence: In the centre of Amsterdam, they painted a small number of used bikes white.
sentences.translation: 他们在阿姆斯特丹市中心把少量二手自行车漆成白色。

structure.text: painted a small number of used bikes white

difficult_points: 句首介词短语交代动作发生地点，不是从句。
painted ... white 是“动词 + 宾语 + 宾补”结构，white 描述宾语变化后的状态。

sentences.sentence: They also distributed leaflets describing the dangers of cars and inviting people to use the white bikes.
sentences.translation: 他们还分发传单，说明汽车的危害，并邀请人们使用这些白色自行车。

structure.text: describing the dangers of cars and inviting people to use the white bikes

difficult_points: describing 和 inviting 是并列的现在分词，修饰 leaflets 的内容和用途。
to use the white bikes 是不定式短语，作 inviting people 的补足内容。

sentences.sentence: The bikes were then left unlocked at various locations around the city, to be used by anyone in need of transport.
sentences.translation: 随后，这些自行车被不上锁地留在城市各处，供任何需要交通工具的人使用。

structure.text: at various locations around the city
to be used by anyone in need of transport

difficult_points: were left unlocked 是被动结构，unlocked 是宾语补足语转化来的主语补足状态。
to be used by anyone 表目的，说明把车留在各处的用途。
in need of transport 后置修饰 anyone，表示“需要交通工具的人”。

sentences.sentence: Luud Schimmelpennink, a Dutch industrial engineer who still lives and cycles in Amsterdam, was heavily involved in the original scheme.
sentences.translation: Luud Schimmelpennink 是一位至今仍在阿姆斯特丹生活并骑车的荷兰工业工程师，他深度参与了最初的计划。

structure.text: a Dutch industrial engineer who still lives and cycles in Amsterdam
was heavily involved in the original scheme

difficult_points: a Dutch industrial engineer 是同位语，补充说明人物身份。
who still lives and cycles in Amsterdam 是定语从句，说明这位工程师与阿姆斯特丹的持续联系。
was heavily involved in 表示“深度参与”，不是简单的“被卷入”。

sentences.sentence: He recalls how the scheme succeeded in attracting a great deal of attention—particularly when it came to publicising Provo's aims—but struggled to get off the ground.
sentences.translation: 他回忆说，这一计划成功吸引了大量关注——尤其是在宣传 Provo 的目标方面——但在真正启动实施上却困难重重。

structure.text: how the scheme succeeded in attracting a great deal of attention—particularly when it came to publicising Provo's aims—but struggled to get off the ground
when it came to publicising Provo's aims

difficult_points: how 引导 recalls 的宾语从句，概括计划成功与受挫的两个方面。
破折号中的 particularly when ... 是插入说明，强调吸引关注主要体现在宣传目标上。
get off the ground 是习语，表示项目真正启动或开始运转。

sentences.sentence: The police were opposed to Provo's initiatives and almost as soon as the white bikes were distributed around the city, they removed them.
sentences.translation: 警方反对 Provo 的倡议，而且几乎在白色自行车刚被分发到城市各处时，就把它们移走了。

structure.text: almost as soon as the white bikes were distributed around the city

difficult_points: were opposed to 表示“反对”，主语是 The police。
as soon as 引导时间状语从句，说明移走自行车发生得非常快。
they 和 them 分别指代警方和白色自行车，指代关系要分清。

sentences.sentence: However, for Schimmelpennink and for bike-sharing schemes in general, this was just the beginning.
sentences.translation: 然而，对 Schimmelpennink 以及总体上的自行车共享计划来说，这仅仅是个开始。

structure.text: for Schimmelpennink and for bike-sharing schemes in general

difficult_points: However 标记转折，说明前面的失败并非故事终点。
for ... and for ... 并列两个适用对象，表示“对……而言”。
this 指代前文最早的白色自行车计划及其遭遇。

sentences.sentence: 'The first Witte Fietsenplan was just a symbolic thing,' he says.
sentences.translation: 他说：“第一个 Witte Fietsenplan 只是一件象征性的事情。”
difficult_points: 引号内是直接引语，he says 是引语后的报道分句。
just a symbolic thing 强调这个计划更重在象征意义，而非实际运营规模。

sentences.sentence: 'We painted a few bikes white, that was all.
sentences.translation: “我们只是把几辆自行车漆成白色，仅此而已。
difficult_points: painted a few bikes white 仍是“动词 + 宾语 + 宾补”结构。
that was all 表示“仅此而已”，强调行动规模很有限。

sentences.sentence: Things got more serious when I became a member of the Amsterdam city council two years later.'
sentences.translation: 两年后我成为阿姆斯特丹市议会议员时，事情变得更严肃了。”

structure.text: when I became a member of the Amsterdam city council two years later

difficult_points: Things 泛指局势或事情的发展，不指具体物品。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明计划变得更严肃的时间背景。
two years later 修饰 became，指首次计划之后两年。

expressions.meaning_zh: 变得更严肃；进入更认真阶段

sentences.sentence: Schimmelpennink seized this opportunity to present a more elaborate Witte Fietsenplan to the city council.
sentences.translation: Schimmelpennink 抓住这个机会，向市议会提交了一个更详尽的 Witte Fietsenplan。

structure.text: to present a more elaborate Witte Fietsenplan to the city council

difficult_points: seized this opportunity 表示主动抓住机会，承接前文成为市议会议员。
to present ... 是目的状语，说明抓住机会要做什么。
more elaborate 表示方案比早期象征性行动更详细、更成体系。

sentences.sentence: 'My idea was that the municipality of Amsterdam would distribute 10,000 white bikes over the city, for everyone to use,' he explains.
sentences.translation: 他解释说：“我的想法是，阿姆斯特丹市政府将在全城投放一万辆白色自行车，供所有人使用。”

structure.text: that the municipality of Amsterdam would distribute 10,000 white bikes over the city, for everyone to use

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，具体说明 idea 的内容。
would distribute 表示计划中的做法，而不是已经发生的事实。
for everyone to use 是不定式复合结构，说明投放自行车的用途。

sentences.sentence: 'I made serious calculations.
sentences.translation: “我做了认真的测算。
difficult_points: serious calculations 强调方案经过严肃测算，不是随意设想。
这是直接引语的前半部分，引号会在后一句结束。

sentences.sentence: It turned out that a white bicycle—per person, per kilometer—would cost the municipality only 10% of what it contributed to public transport per person per kilometre.'
sentences.translation: 结果表明，按每人每公里计算，一辆白色自行车只会花费市政府对公共交通每人每公里补贴额的10%。

structure.text: that a white bicycle—per person, per kilometer—would cost the municipality only 10% of what it contributed to public transport per person per kilometre
what it contributed to public transport per person per kilometre

difficult_points: It turned out that ... 表示“结果证明……”，it 是形式主语。
破折号中的 per person, per kilometer 插入说明计算口径。
what it contributed to public transport 是 what 引导的名词性从句，作介词 of 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, the council unanimously rejected the plan.
sentences.translation: 然而，市议会一致否决了这个计划。
difficult_points: Nevertheless 标记让步转折，说明尽管成本测算有利，结果仍被否决。
unanimously 表示“一致地”，说明并非少数反对。

sentences.sentence: 'They said that the bicycle belongs to the past.
sentences.translation: “他们说，自行车属于过去。

structure.text: that the bicycle belongs to the past

difficult_points: They 指前一句中的 the council，即市议会成员。
belongs to the past 表示被认为已经过时，而不是字面“属于过去”。

sentences.sentence: They saw a glorious future for the car,' says Schimmelpennink.
sentences.translation: 他们认为汽车有着光辉的未来，”Schimmelpennink 说。

structure.text: saw a glorious future for the car

difficult_points: saw a glorious future for the car 表示他们看好汽车的发展前景。
says Schimmelpennink 是倒装式报道分句，主语在动词后面。

sentences.sentence: But he was not in the least discouraged.
sentences.translation: 但他一点也没有气馁。

structure.text: was not in the least discouraged

difficult_points: But 与前面计划被否决形成转折。
not in the least 是强否定表达，表示“一点也不”。

sentences.sentence: Schimmelpennink never stopped believing in bike-sharing, and in the mid-90s, two Danes asked for his help to set up a system in Copenhagen.
sentences.translation: Schimmelpennink 从未停止相信自行车共享；20世纪90年代中期，两名丹麦人请他帮忙在哥本哈根建立一个系统。

structure.text: and in the mid-90s, two Danes asked for his help to set up a system in Copenhagen

difficult_points: never stopped believing in 强调信念持续存在。
and 连接两个独立分句，后一分句主语是 two Danes。
to set up a system in Copenhagen 说明请求帮助的目的。

sentences.sentence: The result was the world's first large-scale bike-share programme.
sentences.translation: 其结果就是世界上第一个大规模自行车共享项目。

structure.text: was the world's first large-scale bike-share programme

difficult_points: The result 承接上一句两名丹麦人请求帮助建立系统这件事。
large-scale 修饰 bike-share programme，突出规模不同于早期象征性实验。

sentences.sentence: It worked on a deposit: 'You dropped a coin in the bike and when you returned it, you got your money back.'
sentences.translation: 它采用押金机制：“你往车里投一枚硬币，归还自行车时就能拿回钱。”

structure.text: You dropped a coin in the bike and when you returned it, you got your money back

difficult_points: It 指上一句的 bike-share programme。
work on a deposit 表示按押金机制运作。
when you returned it 是时间状语从句，it 指自行车。

sentences.sentence: After setting up the Danish system, Schimmelpennink decided to try his luck again in the Netherlands—and this time he succeeded in arousing the interest of the Dutch Ministry of Transport.
sentences.translation: 建立丹麦系统之后，Schimmelpennink 决定再次在荷兰碰碰运气——这一次他成功引起了荷兰交通部的兴趣。

structure.text: After setting up the Danish system
decided to try his luck again in the Netherlands
and this time he succeeded in arousing the interest of the Dutch Ministry of Transport

difficult_points: After setting up ... 是现在分词短语，表示时间背景。
try his luck again 表示再次尝试，不是字面“试运气”那么随意。
succeeded in arousing the interest of ... 表示成功引起某机构兴趣。

sentences.sentence: 'Times had changed,' he recalls.
sentences.translation: 他回忆道：“时代已经变了。”
difficult_points: had changed 用过去完成时，表示在他再次尝试之前时代已经发生变化。
Times 指社会环境和公众观念，而不是具体的“时间次数”。

sentences.sentence: 'People had become more environmentally conscious, and the Danish experiment had proved that bike-sharing was a real possibility.'
sentences.translation: “人们已经变得更有环保意识，而丹麦的实验也已经证明，自行车共享是真正可行的。”

structure.text: and the Danish experiment had proved that bike-sharing was a real possibility
that bike-sharing was a real possibility

difficult_points: had become 和 had proved 都用过去完成时，说明这些变化发生在荷兰新计划之前。
environmentally conscious 表示有环保意识，不只是“意识到环境”。
that 引导 proved 的宾语从句，说明丹麦实验证明的内容。

sentences.sentence: A new Witte Fietsenplan was launched in 1999 in Amsterdam.
sentences.translation: 1999年，一个新的 Witte Fietsenplan 在阿姆斯特丹启动。
difficult_points: was launched 是被动语态，强调计划被启动。
in 1999 和 in Amsterdam 分别交代时间和地点。

sentences.sentence: However, riding a white bike was no longer free; it cost one guilder per trip and payment was made with a chip card developed by the Dutch bank Postbank.
sentences.translation: 不过，骑白色自行车不再免费；每次行程要花一荷兰盾，并且通过荷兰银行 Postbank 开发的芯片卡付款。

structure.text: it cost one guilder per trip
payment was made with a chip card developed by the Dutch bank Postbank

difficult_points: riding a white bike 是动名词短语作主语。
分号连接两个紧密相关的说明：不免费以及如何付款。
developed by the Dutch bank Postbank 是过去分词短语，修饰 chip card。

sentences.sentence: Schimmelpennink designed conspicuous, sturdy white bikes locked in special racks which could be opened with the chip card—the plan started with 250 bikes, distributed over five stations.
sentences.translation: Schimmelpennink 设计了醒目而结实的白色自行车，它们锁在可用芯片卡打开的专用车架上——该计划开始时有250辆自行车，分布在5个站点。

structure.text: locked in special racks which could be opened with the chip card
the plan started with 250 bikes, distributed over five stations

difficult_points: locked in special racks 是过去分词短语，修饰 white bikes。
which could be opened with the chip card 是定语从句，修饰 special racks。
破折号后补充计划初始规模，distributed over five stations 修饰 250 bikes。

sentences.sentence: Theo Molenaar, who was a system designer for the project, worked alongside Schimmelpennink.
sentences.translation: Theo Molenaar 是该项目的一名系统设计师，他与 Schimmelpennink 一起工作。

structure.text: who was a system designer for the project

difficult_points: who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充 Theo Molenaar 的身份。
worked alongside 表示并肩合作，而不仅是地点上“在旁边工作”。

sentences.sentence: 'I remember when we were testing the bike racks, he announced that he had already designed better ones.
sentences.translation: “我记得我们测试自行车架时，他宣布自己已经设计出了更好的车架。

structure.text: when we were testing the bike racks
he announced that he had already designed better ones

difficult_points: when we were testing the bike racks 交代记忆中的时间场景。
that he had already designed better ones 是 announced 的宾语从句。
ones 替代前面的 bike racks，避免重复。

expressions.meaning_zh: 设计出更好的同类产品

sentences.sentence: But of course, we had to go through with the ones we had.'
sentences.translation: 但当然，我们不得不继续使用已经有的那些。”

structure.text: had to go through with the ones we had

difficult_points: But of course 标记转折，说明即便有更好设计也无法立刻更换。
go through with 表示坚持完成或继续推进某事。
the ones we had 中省略关系代词，we had 修饰 ones。

sentences.sentence: The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft.
sentences.translation: 然而，这个系统容易遭到故意破坏和盗窃。

structure.text: was prone to vandalism and theft

difficult_points: however 插入主语和谓语之间，表示与前面对设计推进的叙述形成转折。
be prone to 表示“容易受到/倾向于发生”，后接名词 vandalism and theft。

sentences.sentence: 'After every weekend there would always be a couple of bikes missing,' Molenaar says.
sentences.translation: Molenaar 说：“每个周末之后，总会有几辆自行车不见。”

structure.text: there would always be a couple of bikes missing

difficult_points: there would always be 是存在句，would 表示过去反复发生的情况。
a couple of bikes missing 中 missing 作后置状态补足，表示自行车不见了。
After every weekend 是介词短语，不是从句。

sentences.sentence: 'I really have no idea what people did with them, because they could instantly be recognised as white bikes.'
sentences.translation: “我真的不知道人们拿它们做了什么，因为它们一眼就能被认出是白色自行车。”

structure.text: because they could instantly be recognised as white bikes

difficult_points: what people did with them 是名词性从句，作 idea 的内容补足。
they 和 them 都指丢失的白色自行车。
could instantly be recognised as 是被动结构，表示很容易立刻被认出来。

sentences.sentence: But the biggest blow came when Postbank decided to abolish the chip card, because it wasn't profitable.
sentences.translation: 但最大的打击出现在 Postbank 决定取消芯片卡时，因为它并不盈利。

structure.text: when Postbank decided to abolish the chip card

difficult_points: the biggest blow 是抽象主语，指对项目影响最大的挫折。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明打击发生的节点。
because it wasn't profitable 中 it 指 chip card 或相关芯片卡业务。

sentences.sentence: 'That chip card was pivotal to the system,' Molenaar says.
sentences.translation: Molenaar 说：“那张芯片卡对这个系统至关重要。”
difficult_points: pivotal to 表示对某事具有核心作用，比 important 更强调关键性。
That chip card 指上一句 Postbank 决定取消的芯片卡。

sentences.sentence: 'To continue the project we would have needed to set up another system, but the business partner had lost interest.'
sentences.translation: “为了继续这个项目，我们本来需要建立另一个系统，但商业伙伴已经失去了兴趣。”

structure.text: would have needed to set up another system
but the business partner had lost interest

difficult_points: To continue the project 是目的状语，说明另建系统的原因。
would have needed to 表示过去条件下本来会需要做的事。
had lost interest 用过去完成时，表示商业伙伴在继续推进前已经不感兴趣。

sentences.sentence: Schimmelpennink was disappointed, but—characteristically—not for long.
sentences.translation: Schimmelpennink 很失望，但这很符合他的性格——失望并没有持续多久。
difficult_points: but 后省略了 he was disappointed，not for long 表示失望没有持续太久。
characteristically 是插入语，说明这种很快振作符合他的典型性格。
破折号突出插入评价，阅读时不要把它误作主干。

expressions.meaning_zh: 典型地；符合一贯性格地

sentences.sentence: In 2002 he got a call from the French advertising corporation JCDecaux, who wanted to set up his bike-sharing scheme in Vienna.
sentences.translation: 2002年，他接到法国广告公司 JCDecaux 的电话；这家公司想在维也纳建立他的自行车共享计划。

structure.text: got a call from the French advertising corporation JCDecaux
who wanted to set up his bike-sharing scheme in Vienna

difficult_points: who 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 corporation JCDecaux。
his bike-sharing scheme 中 his 指 Schimmelpennink，说明该方案源于他的构想。
set up ... in Vienna 说明公司联系他的目的。

sentences.sentence: 'That went really well.
sentences.translation: “那进展得非常顺利。
difficult_points: That 指在维也纳建立的自行车共享系统。
went really well 表示项目进展顺利，而不是字面“走得好”。

sentences.sentence: After Vienna, they set up a system in Lyon.
sentences.translation: 继维也纳之后，他们又在里昂建立了一个系统。

structure.text: set up a system in Lyon

difficult_points: After Vienna 表示继维也纳之后，是时间和顺序提示。
they 指 JCDecaux 等实施方，而不是所有城市。

sentences.sentence: Then in 2007, Paris followed.
sentences.translation: 随后在2007年，巴黎也跟进了。
difficult_points: Paris followed 是拟人化表达，指巴黎也采用了类似系统。
Then in 2007 给出时间顺序，承接维也纳和里昂之后的发展。

sentences.sentence: That was a decisive moment in the history of bike-sharing.'
sentences.translation: 那是自行车共享历史上的决定性时刻。”
difficult_points: That 指巴黎跟进建立共享单车系统这件事。
decisive moment 表示具有转折意义的关键时刻。
in the history of bike-sharing 限定这个时刻的重要性所在的领域。

sentences.sentence: The huge and unexpected success of the Parisian bike-sharing programme, which now boasts more than 20,000 bicycles, inspired cities all over the world to set up their own schemes, all modelled on Schimmelpennink's.
sentences.translation: 巴黎自行车共享项目取得了巨大而出人意料的成功，如今它拥有两万多辆自行车；这一成功激励了世界各地的城市建立自己的计划，而这些计划都以 Schimmelpennink 的方案为蓝本。

structure.text: The huge and unexpected success of the Parisian bike-sharing programme
which now boasts more than 20,000 bicycles
inspired cities all over the world to set up their own schemes

difficult_points: 主语很长，核心是 success，of 短语说明是哪一个项目的成功。
which now boasts more than 20,000 bicycles 是非限制性定语从句，补充巴黎项目规模。
all modelled on Schimmelpennink's 是过去分词短语，说明各城市方案的共同参照。

sentences.sentence: 'It's wonderful that this happened,' he says.
sentences.translation: 他说：“这件事发生了，真是太好了。”
difficult_points: It's wonderful that ... 中 It 是形式主语，真正评价对象是 that 从句。
this happened 指他的共享单车理念在世界范围内扩散。
he says 是报道分句，标明引语来源。

sentences.sentence: 'But financially I didn't really benefit from it, because I never filed for a patent.'
sentences.translation: “但在经济上我并没有真正从中获益，因为我从未申请专利。”

structure.text: because I never filed for a patent

difficult_points: financially 限定获益的方面，说明不是否认精神或声誉层面的意义。
benefit from it 中 it 指全球共享单车方案的扩散。
because 引导原因状语从句，说明未获经济收益的原因。

sentences.sentence: In Amsterdam today, 38% of all trips are made by bike and, along with Copenhagen, it is regarded as one of the two most cycle-friendly capitals in the world—but the city never got another Witte Fietsenplan.
sentences.translation: 如今在阿姆斯特丹，38%的出行由自行车完成；它与哥本哈根一起被认为是世界上两个最适合骑行的首都之一——但这座城市再也没有获得另一个 Witte Fietsenplan。

structure.text: it is regarded as one of the two most cycle-friendly capitals in the world
but the city never got another Witte Fietsenplan

difficult_points: are made by bike 是被动结构，表示出行方式。
it 指 Amsterdam，而不是 Copenhagen。
破折号后 but 引出反差：骑行友好却没有新的白色自行车计划。

sentences.sentence: Molenaar believes this may be because everybody in Amsterdam already has a bike.
sentences.translation: Molenaar 认为，这可能是因为阿姆斯特丹每个人都已经有自行车。

structure.text: this may be because everybody in Amsterdam already has a bike
because everybody in Amsterdam already has a bike

difficult_points: believes 后省略 that，引出宾语从句。
this 指阿姆斯特丹没有再获得白色自行车计划这件事。
may be because 表示推测原因，不是确定结论。

sentences.sentence: Schimmelpennink, however, cannot see that this changes Amsterdam's need for a bike-sharing scheme.
sentences.translation: 然而，Schimmelpennink 并不认为这会改变阿姆斯特丹对自行车共享计划的需求。

structure.text: that this changes Amsterdam's need for a bike-sharing scheme

difficult_points: however 插入主语后，表示与 Molenaar 的解释相反。
cannot see that ... 表示“不认为/看不出……”，不是视觉意义。
this 指“人人已有自行车”这一情况。

sentences.sentence: 'People who travel on the underground don't carry their bikes around.
sentences.translation: “乘坐地铁的人不会随身带着他们的自行车。

structure.text: People who travel on the underground

difficult_points: who travel on the underground 是定语从句，限定 People 的范围。
carry their bikes around 表示随身携带自行车四处走。
underground 在英式英语中指地铁。

sentences.sentence: But often they need additional transport to reach their final destination.'
sentences.translation: 但他们往往需要额外的交通工具才能到达最终目的地。”
difficult_points: they 指上一句中乘坐地铁的人。
additional transport 表示地铁之外的补充交通方式。
to reach their final destination 表目的，说明需要额外交通的原因。

sentences.sentence: Although he thinks it is strange that a city like Amsterdam does not have a successful bike-sharing scheme, he is optimistic about the future.
sentences.translation: 虽然他认为像阿姆斯特丹这样的城市没有成功的自行车共享计划很奇怪，但他对未来持乐观态度。

structure.text: Although he thinks it is strange that a city like Amsterdam does not have a successful bike-sharing scheme
it is strange that a city like Amsterdam does not have a successful bike-sharing scheme

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，主句是 he is optimistic about the future。
thinks 后的 it is strange that ... 中 it 是形式主语，真正内容是 that 从句。
a city like Amsterdam 强调阿姆斯特丹这样骑行条件优越的城市。

sentences.sentence: 'In the '60s we didn't stand a chance because people were prepared to give their lives to keep cars in the city.
sentences.translation: “在20世纪60年代，我们毫无机会，因为人们愿意付出生命也要把汽车留在城市里。

structure.text: because people were prepared to give their lives to keep cars in the city

difficult_points: stand a chance 是习语，表示“有机会成功”。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释当年没有机会的社会心理原因。
to give their lives to keep cars in the city 带有夸张色彩，强调人们当时维护汽车地位的强烈态度。

sentences.sentence: But that mentality has totally changed.
sentences.translation: 但那种心态已经完全改变了。
difficult_points: But 标记与20世纪60年代的情况形成转折。
that mentality 指前一句中人们拼命保留汽车的心态。
has changed 用现在完成时，强调变化影响延续到现在。

sentences.sentence: Today everybody longs for cities that are not dominated by cars.'
sentences.translation: 如今，每个人都向往不由汽车主导的城市。”

structure.text: that are not dominated by cars

difficult_points: longs for 表示强烈向往，不只是普通想要。
that are not dominated by cars 是定语从句，修饰 cities。
be dominated by cars 表示城市空间和交通被汽车主导。
